top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 83, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
111ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Race-pace efforts that mimic L'Étape Parma's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
At L'Étape Parma, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
L'Étape Parma's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 83 miles.
9,186ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of L'Étape Parma. Train it until it feels boring.
9,186ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 83 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At L'Étape Parma, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mixed terrain at tempo for 83 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.

race_overlay.heat: May in Parma, Emilia-Romagna, Italy can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before L'Étape Parma. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for L'Étape Parma’s 83 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at L'Étape Parma. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at L'Étape Parma requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 83 miles of mixed terrain in Parma, Emilia-Romagna, Italy.
